info.title: AIForce Governance, Risk and Compliance APIs
info.description: GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) ensures organizations operate ethically,         manage risks, and meet regulatory requirements.

get.description:
An example protected API endpoint that requires a valid access token.
This demonstrates how a token generated via `/gettoken` can be used to authenticate requests.

Retrieves the configuration for the authenticated organization and project,
or a subset specified by a heading. Requires a valid access token.

schema.description: Optional: Filter by whether the metric is LLM-based

schema.description: Optional: Filter by whether the metric is custom
schema.title: Custom

parameters.description: Optional: Filter by whether the metric is custom

example.message: 1 metric configuration(s) were reset to default.

post.description:
Creates a new custom metric for the given organization and project.

Flow:
1. Validate organization and project from access token.
2. Check for duplicate metric names.
3. If non-LLM metric:
    - Create or reuse shared Python virtual environment
    - Install required packages
    - Persist metric with script-based evaluation
4. If LLM-based metric:
    - Persist metric with model configuration
5. Return success response with metric details.

Raises:
    HTTPException (400): For validation or duplicate errors.
    HTTPException (500): For internal server errors.

post.description:
Create a new dataset.

This endpoint allows you to create a new dataset by specifying a unique name
and an optional description.

🚫 Note:
- Dataset name must be 3–100 characters long and cannot contain special characters.
- If a dataset with the same name already exists, an error will be returned.
- use_case_type can be prompt, rag or agent only

📘 Example request body:
{
    "name": "financial-fraud-detection",
    "description": "Dataset containing fraud detection examples"
}

✅ Returns: Dataset ID and name on successful creation.

get.description:
List all datasets.

Retrieves a list of all datasets created in Langfuse.

✅ Response includes:
- Dataset ID
- Name
- Description (if any)
- Applicability
- Usecase type
- No of items
- Created timestamp
- Updated timestamp

📘 Useful for:
- Getting dataset names for adding new items
- Verifying if a dataset already exists
- Filtering based on applicability and use case
- Getting datasets based on page number and no of records per page

parameters.name: time

schema.description: Filter datasets by time they are created (either 'Past 24 hours', 'Past 3 days', 'Past 1 Week' or 'Past 1 Month').
schema.title: Time

parameters.description: Filter datasets by time they are created (either 'Past 24 hours', 'Past 3 days', 'Past 1 Week' or 'Past 1 Month').

parameters.name: name

schema.description: Filter datasets by name they start with.
schema.title: Name

parameters.description: Filter datasets by name they start with.

get.description:
Download a dataset template file based on the given use case type.

This endpoint validates the authenticated user, verifies that the provided
use_case_type is supported, and returns the corresponding dataset template
file as a downloadable response.

Args:
    use_case_type (str): The use case type for which the dataset template
        is requested. Must be one of the allowed values defined in
        VALID_USE_CASE_TYPES.
    current_user_payload (dict): Authenticated user information extracted
        from the access token.

Raises:
    HTTPException:
        - 400 if the user is not authenticated or the use_case_type is invalid.
        - 404 if the dataset template file does not exist.

Returns:
    FileResponse: A streaming file response containing the dataset template.

post.description:
Upload a dataset file and persist dataset items.

Supports CSV, XLS, and XLSX formats with validation based on use case.

get.description: Retrieves all items belonging to a specified Langfuse dataset.

Retrieves a single dataset item by its unique identifier.

This method fetches the details of a specific dataset item from a Langfuse dataset
using the provided `item_id`. Dataset items typically include inputs,
expected outputs, and associated metadata, which are essential for
evaluation and analysis of LLM application runs.

Args:
    - item_id (str): The unique identifier of the dataset item to retrieve.

Returns:
    DatasetItemResponse: A response object containing the details of the
                         requested dataset item.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the dataset item with the specified ID is not found.

put.description: Updates a dataset item by its unique identifier.

delete.description: Deletes a single dataset item by its unique identifier (async version).
Asynchronously deletes a dataset by its name.

get.description:
Retrieve a trace by its ID for the authenticated organization and project.
Requires a valid access token.

Args:
    - trace_id (str): Unique identifier of the trace.

Returns:
    - TraceResponse: Trace data if found, else an HTTP exception.

delete.description:
Remove a trace by its ID for the authenticated organization and project.
Requires a valid access token.

Args:
    - trace_id (str): Unique identifier of the trace.

Returns:
    - dict: Message confirming the trace deletion.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all traces for the authenticated organization and project,
with optional filtering and pagination.

post.description:
Run manual LLM evaluation for prompt testing.

This endpoint validates prompt inputs, execution and evaluation configuration, and
executes an LLM-based evaluation workflow.

Args:
    request (PromptTestRequest): The prompt test request body.
    current_user_payload (dict): The current user's access token payload.

Returns:
    TestResponse: The response from the LLM evaluation.

Run manual LLM evaluation for Agent testing.

This endpoint validates agent prompt inputs, execution and evaluation
configuration, and executes an LLM-based agent evaluation workflow.

example.prompt:
Evaluation Prompt - Evaluate the degree of hallucination in the following generation on a continuous scale from 8 to 1. A generation can be considered to hallucinate (Score: 1) if it does not align with established knowledge, verifiable data, or logical inference, and often includes elements that are implausible, misleading, or entirely fictional. Respond ONLY in this format:
Score: <number from 1 to 8>
Status: Pass or Fail
A wiki page containing info related to Taj Mahal

post.description:
Run manual LLM evaluation for RAG testing.

This endpoint validates prompt, retrieved context, and evaluation
configuration before executing a RAG-based LLM evaluation.

example.prompt:
Evaluation Prompt - Evaluate the degree of hallucination in the following generation on a continuous scale from 8 to 1. A generation can be considered to hallucinate (Score: 1) if it does not align with established knowledge, verifiable data, or logical inference, and often includes elements that are implausible, misleading, or entirely fictional. Respond ONLY in this format:
Score: <number from 1 to 8>
Status: Pass or Fail
A wiki page containing info related to Taj Mahal
example.retrieved_context: A wiki page containing info related to Taj Mahal

post.description:
API to evaluate vulnerabilities in the provided Python script and packages using LLM.
Args:
    script (str): Python code to evaluate.
    packages (list[str]): List of package names.
Returns:
    dict: Vulnerability evaluation results.

trace_id.title: Trace Id
trace_id.description: Trace ID to which the event is attached

name.title: Name
name.description: Identifier of the span/event. Useful for sorting/filtering in the UI.

start_time.title: Start Time
start_time.description: The time at which the span started, defaults to the current time

end_time.title: End Time
end_time.description: The time at which the span ended

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Additional metadata of the span. Can be any JSON object

level.title: Level
level.description: The level of the span. Can be DEBUG, DEFAULT, WARNING, or ERROR

status_message.title: Status Message
status_message.description: Additional field for context, e.g. error message

input.title: Input
input.description: Input to the span. Can be any JSON object

output.title: Output
output.description: Output from the span. Can be any JSON object

version.title: Version
version.description: Version of the span type. Useful for debugging and metrics

AgentEvaluationRequest.title: AgentEvaluationRequest
AgentEvaluationRequest.description:
Request model for evaluating an agent with multiple metric-dataset pairs.

Attributes:

    metric_datasets: List of metrics mapped to datasets.
    execution_platform: Platform to execute agent.
    execution_model: Model to generate responses.
    evaluation_platform: Platform for evaluation.
    evaluation_model: Model used for evaluation.

AgentTestRequest.title: AgentTestRequest
AgentTestRequest.description:
Request schema for evaluating an agent's response across platforms.

This model is intended for agent-based evaluations where execution
and evaluation platforms/models are mandatory.

example.prompt:
Evaluation Prompt - Evaluate the degree of hallucination in the following generation on a continuous scale from 8 to 1. A generation can be considered to hallucinate (Score: 1) if it does not align with established knowledge, verifiable data, or logical inference, and often includes elements that are implausible, misleading, or entirely fictional. Respond ONLY in this format:
Score: <number from 1 to 8>
Status: Pass or Fail
A wiki page containing info related to Taj Mahal

ComplianceScanResult.title: ComplianceScanResult
ComplianceScanResult.description:
Aggregated compliance scan results for a single request.

Attributes:
    request_id (str): Request ID associated with the scan.
    results (List[ComplianceStatusSchema]): List of individual compliance results.
    aggregated_compliance_recommendation (Optional[Dict[str, str]]): Consolidated
    recommendations per compliance ID.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique ID for the evaluation task

status.title: Status
status.description: Current status of the evaluation task (pending, running, success, failed)

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Time the evaluation task was created

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: Time the evaluation task was last updated

ComplianceStatusSchema.title: ComplianceStatusSchema
ComplianceStatusSchema.description:
Schema representing status and result of a single compliance evaluation.

Attributes:
    id (str): Unique ID for the evaluation task.
    request_id (str): Request ID.
    org_id (str): Organization ID.
    project_id (str): Project ID.
    prompt_id (str): Prompt ID used for evaluation.
    prompt_type (Optional[str]): Type of prompt (system/user).
    compliance_id (Optional[str]): Compliance ID.
    compliance_name (Optional[str]): Name of compliance check.
    description (Optional[str]): Description of compliance.
    problem (Optional[str]): Problem identified by compliance check.
    why_it_failed (Optional[str]): Reason for failure.
    compliance_recommendation (Optional[str]): Suggested rephrased prompt.
    grade (Optional[str]): Grade assigned during evaluation.
    threshold (Optional[str]): Threshold for passing.
    passed (Optional[str]): Whether compliance passed or failed.
    status (str): Current evaluation status (pending, running, success, failed).
    created_at (datetime): Creation timestamp.
    updated_at (datetime): Last updated timestamp.

InlineDatasetItem.title: InlineDatasetItem
InlineDatasetItem.description:
Represents a single inline dataset item used for metric evaluation.

The item can either provide:
- prompt variables to expand a global prompt, OR
- a fully resolved sanitized prompt.

PromptDatasetValidationRequest.description:
Request schema for validating a prompt against one or more metrics
using datasets or inline test cases.

This is typically used for design-time prompt evaluation.

PromptDatasetValidationResponse.description: Final response returned after prompt dataset validation.

PromptTestRequest.title: PromptTestRequest
PromptTestRequest.description:
Request schema for evaluating a single prompt against expected outputs.

This model is intended for evaluating prompts where a single textual generation
is tested against an expected output using optional scoring thresholds.

example.prompt:
Evaluation Prompt - Evaluate the degree of hallucination in the following generation on a continuous scale from 8 to 1. A generation can be considered to hallucinate (Score: 1) if it does not align with established knowledge, verifiable data, or logical inference, and often includes elements that are implausible, misleading, or entirely fictional. Respond ONLY in this format:
Score: <number from 1 to 8>
Status: Pass or Fail
A wiki page containing info related to Taj Mahal

RAGTestRequest.title: RAGTestRequest
RAGTestRequest.description:
Request schema for evaluating RAG-based prompts with retrieval context.

This model adds `retrieved_context` for evaluating responses that depend
on external document retrieval, while including execution/evaluation metadata.

example.prompt:
Evaluation Prompt - Evaluate the degree of hallucination in the following generation on a continuous scale from 8 to 1. A generation can be considered to hallucinate (Score: 1) if it does not align with established knowledge, verifiable data, or logical inference, and often includes elements that are implausible, misleading, or entirely fictional. Respond ONLY in this format:
Score: <number from 1 to 8>
Status: Pass or Fail
A wiki page containing info related to Taj Mahal
example.retrieved_context: A wiki page containing info related to Taj Mahal

prompt_name.title: Prompt Name
prompt_name.description: Unique name of the prompt to be validated.

input_prompt.description: Prompt template containing placeholders to be resolved during execution.

Please summarize the following information for a 5th grader, focusing on the main points: {{Context}}

User Question: {{Question}}.

system_prompt.description: Optional system-level instruction provided to the model.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the validation request.

top_n_rerank.title: Top N Rerank
top_n_rerank.description: Number of top documents retained after reranking.

metric_datasets.title: Metric Datasets
metric_datasets.description: List of metric-to-dataset mappings used for evaluation.

evaluation_platform.title: Evaluation Platform
evaluation_platform.description: Platform used to evaluate the metrics.

execution_platform.description: Platform used to execute the prompt.

execution_model.title: Execution Model
execution_model.description: Model identifier used to execute the prompt.

RagDatasetValidationRequest.title: RagDatasetValidationRequest
RagDatasetValidationRequest.description:
Request schema for validating RAG-based prompts using datasets.

This request evaluates:
- Retrieval quality
- Reranking effectiveness
- Response correctness

using one or more evaluation metrics mapped to datasets.

example.input_prompt:
Please summarize the following information for a 5th grader, focusing on the main points: {{Context}}

User Question: {{Question}}.
example.system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant that ensures all user inputs are anonymized before responding.

RagDatasetValidationResponse.title: RagDatasetValidationResponse
RagDatasetValidationResponse.description: Response schema returned after submitting a RAG dataset validation request.

system_prompt.title: System Prompt
system_prompt.description: The system prompt text to be evaluated

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The prompt text to be evaluated for compliance

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: List of documents to be analyzed

ScanRequest.title: ScanRequest
ScanRequest.description:
Request model for scanning a prompt and associated documents.

Attributes:
    prompt_name (Optional[str]): Unique identifier for the prompt.
    system_prompt (Optional[str]): System prompt text to be evaluated.
    prompt (str): User prompt text to be evaluated for compliance.
    documents (Optional[List[str]]): List of documents to analyze.
    evaluation_platform (Optional[str]): LLM evaluation platform.
    evaluation_model (Optional[str]): LLM model name.

TestResponse.title: TestResponse
TestResponse.description:
Model representing a test response from an LLM.

Attributes:
    llm_response (Optional[Dict[str, Any]]): The response returned by the LLM,
        represented as a dictionary with string keys and any type of values.

TraceResponse.title: TraceResponse
TraceResponse.description:
Response model returned after creating or fetching a single trace.

Attributes:
    id (str): Unique identifier of the trace.
    input (dict | str, optional): Stringified representation of the input.
    name (str, optional): Name of the trace.
    timestamp (datetime, optional): Timestamp of trace creation.
    environment (str, optional): Runtime environment (Design/Production).
    userId (str, optional): User identifier who created the trace.
    scores (list, optional): List of evaluation scores.
    observations (list, optional): List of observations.
    output (dict | str, optional): Stringified representation of the output.
    metadata (dict): Metadata associated with the trace.
    sessionId (str): Identifier of the session this trace belongs to.
    promptTokens (int, optional): Number of tokens in the prompt.
    completionTokens (int, optional): Number of tokens in the completion.
    totalTokens (int, optional): Total tokens used (prompt + completion).
    latency (float, optional): Latency in seconds.
    totalCost (float, optional): Cost of this trace.

example.input: What is the Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) known for?

contexts: The Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) was established in 1956 following the nationalization of the insurance industry in India.
LIC is the largest insurance company in India, with a vast network of policyholders and huge investments.
As the largest institutional investor in India, LIC manages substantial funds, contributing to the financial stability of the country.
The Indian economy is one of the fastest-growing major economies in the world, thanks to sectors like finance, technology, manufacturing etc.
references: The Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) is the largest insurance company in India, established in 1956 through the nationalization of the insurance industry. It is known for managing a large portfolio of investments.

output.content: The Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) is the largest insurance company in India, known for its vast portfolio of investments. LIC contributes to the financial stability of the country.
output.name: None

TraceWithDetails.title: TraceWithDetails
TraceWithDetails.description:
Model representing a trace with detailed input/output, used in lists.

Attributes:
    id (str): Unique identifier of the trace.
    input (dict | str): The input data provided to the model.
    output (dict | str): The output data generated by the model.

schemas__agent_eval_params__MetricDatase.title: MetricDatasetMapping
schemas__agent_eval_params__MetricDatase.description:
Mapping of a metric to a dataset.

Attributes:
    metric: Name of the metric.
    dataset: Name of the dataset.

dataset.title: Dataset
dataset.description: Dataset name (optional if inline dataset_items is provided)

dataset_items.title: Dataset Items
dataset_items.description: Inline dataset items instead of dataset name

schemas__prompt__MetricDatasetMapping.title: MetricDatasetMapping
schemas__prompt__MetricDatasetMapping.description:
Maps a metric to either a stored dataset or inline dataset items.

Exactly one of `dataset` or `dataset_items` must be provided.

schemas__rag__MetricDatasetMapping.title: MetricDatasetMapping
schemas__rag__MetricDatasetMapping.description: Maps a metric to a dataset used for RAG evaluation.
